title: Turkmenistan Highlights Tour
icon_label: Ashgabat, Darvaza, Ancient Merv, Yangykala Canyon

labels.label: This 7-day Turkmenistan journey is designed for travelers who want to experience the country beyond the surface — from the white marble avenues of Ashgabat to the fire-lit desert night at Darvaza, the ancient Silk Road ruins of Merv, and the dramatic colors of Yangykala Canyon.

The route combines culture, history, desert landscapes, local stories, and carefully planned logistics. It is suitable for private travelers, couples, small groups, and guests who want a guided Central Asia experience with local knowledge and practical support along the way.

details.label: Local English-speaking guide according to the itinerary
Accommodation in selected hotels and desert camp/yurt-style stay where applicable
Entrance tickets for listed sightseeing places
Visa invitation support and travel planning guidance

details.title: Arrival in Ashgabat
details.content: Arrive in Ashgabat and meet your local guide. Transfer to the hotel and begin with a gentle introduction to Turkmenistan’s capital, known for its white marble architecture, wide avenues, monuments, and distinctive city atmosphere.

details.title: Ashgabat City Tour
details.content: Explore the main highlights of Ashgabat, including key monuments, local markets, the National Museum, and architectural landmarks. This day gives context to Turkmenistan’s history, culture, and modern identity.

details.title: Ashgabat – Darvaza Gas Crater
details.content: Drive north through the Karakum Desert toward Darvaza Gas Crater. Arrive before sunset and enjoy the changing desert light. In the evening, experience the famous burning crater under the night sky.

details.title: Darvaza – Ashgabat
details.content: After breakfast in the desert, return to Ashgabat with stops along the way depending on timing and road conditions. The afternoon can include additional sightseeing or time to rest.

details.title: Ashgabat – Ancient Merv – Mary
details.content: Travel to the historic region of Mary and visit Ancient Merv, one of the great cities of the Silk Road and a UNESCO World Heritage site. Explore the remains of fortresses, mausoleums, and old city walls that tell the story of centuries of trade and culture.

details.title: Mary – Yangykala Canyon
details.content: Continue toward the striking landscapes of Yangykala Canyon, known for its layered cliffs and dramatic colors. This is one of Turkmenistan’s most photogenic natural sites and a highlight for landscape lovers.

details.title: Return to Ashgabat / Departure
details.content: Return to Ashgabat for your onward flight or extension. Depending on your schedule, the route can be adjusted with additional stops, extra nights, or connections to Uzbekistan and wider Central Asia.

question.question: Can this tour be customized?
question.answer: Yes. The route can be adjusted based on your travel dates, flight schedule, preferred comfort level, and whether you want to continue to Uzbekistan or another Central Asia destination.

question.question: Do I need visa support for Turkmenistan?
question.answer: Most travelers need visa or invitation support for Turkmenistan. We can guide you through the required documents and help coordinate the travel invitation process where applicable.

question.question: Is Darvaza Gas Crater included?
question.answer: Yes. The itinerary includes an overnight or evening visit to Darvaza Gas Crater, depending on the final route, season, and road conditions.

question.question: Is this tour suitable for first-time visitors?
question.answer: Yes. This route is designed as a strong first introduction to Turkmenistan, combining city culture, Silk Road history, desert scenery, and natural landscapes.

review.label: The route was well planned and the local guide made Turkmenistan much easier to understand. Darvaza at night was unforgettable, and the logistics felt smooth from arrival to departure.
We wanted a Central Asia trip that felt local, not rushed. The balance of Ashgabat, Merv, desert routes, and canyon landscapes was exactly what we hoped for.
